info.title: Qwen3.8-Max-Preview - Responses API
info.description:
OpenAI Responses-compatible API (`/v1/responses`) for Tongyi Qwen3.8-Max-Preview.

**Advantages over Chat Completions**:
- **Simplified context management**: link to the previous turn via `previous_response_id`, without manually assembling the full history
- **Built-in tools**: web search, web extraction, code interpreter, and more
- **Convenient session caching**: add `x-dashscope-session-cache: enable` to the request header, and the server automatically caches the conversation context, reducing multi-turn latency and cost

**Capabilities**: multimodal input (text + image), thinking-effort control (`reasoning.effort`), and streaming output.

servers.description: Production environment (recommended, better long-connection support for text models)
Primary multimodal endpoint (use when input includes images)

post.description:
> 🚧 **This model is not yet available, stay tuned**

- Call Qwen3.8-Max-Preview using the OpenAI Responses protocol
- **Flexible input**: `input` can be a string or a Chat-format message array (supporting multimodal `input_text` / `input_image`)
- **Multi-turn conversation**: pass `previous_response_id` to link to the previous response, and the server automatically assembles the context (the response `id` is valid for 7 days)
- **Session caching**: enable by adding `x-dashscope-session-cache: enable` to the request header (default disable); see `usage.input_tokens_details.cached_tokens` for hits
- **Thinking effort**: controlled via `reasoning.effort`
- **Streaming output**: when `stream=true`, returned as Responses events (`response.output_text.delta` / `response.completed`, etc.)

parameters.name: x-dashscope-session-cache
parameters.description: Session caching switch. When set to `enable`, the server automatically caches the conversation context to reduce multi-turn inference latency and cost.

value.input: How is it different from GBDT?

bearerAuth.description:
##All APIs require Bearer Token authentication##

**Get API Key**:

Visit [API Key Management Page](https://evolink.ai/dashboard/keys) to get your API Key

**Add to request header**:
```
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
```

input.description: Model input. Can be a string (plain text) or a Chat-format message array (supporting multimodal `input_text` / `input_image`).

instructions.description: Inserted as a system instruction at the start of the context. When using `previous_response_id`, the previous turn's `instructions` are not carried into this turn.

previous_response_id.description: Unique ID of the previous response (the response `id`, valid for 7 days). Used to link multi-turn conversations; the server automatically retrieves and combines that turn's input and output as context.

max_output_tokens.description: Maximum number of output tokens to generate this time (including thinking).

store.description:
Whether to store this response.

- `true` (default): can be referenced by `previous_response_id`
- `false`: not stored and cannot be referenced later

stream.description: Whether to return a Responses event stream.

tools.description: List of tools. Supports built-in tools (`web_search` for web search, `web_extractor` for web extraction, `code_interpreter` for code interpretation) and custom `function` tools.

content.description: Message content. Pass a string for plain text; pass a content part array for multimodal input.

InputContentPart.description: Responses input content part; declare the type via `type`.

image_url.description: Image URL when `type=input_image`, or a base64 data URL starting with `data:`

Tool.description: Tool definition. Built-in tools only need to declare `type`; custom functions must provide a name and parameter schema.

id.description: Unique ID of this response (valid for 7 days, can be used as `previous_response_id`)

output.description: Array of output items (including `message` / `reasoning` / built-in tool calls, etc.)

cached_tokens.description: Number of input tokens served from the session cache (cache read)

prompt_tokens_details.description: Cache details returned when session caching is enabled

cached_tokens.description: Number of tokens served from cache

cache_creation_input_tokens.description: Number of tokens newly written to cache in this request (cache write)
